# Table 14-2.03 Exposure Documentation

## Table Title
Summary of Exposure (Safety Population)

## Purpose
This table summarizes the extent of exposure to study treatment, including treatment duration, dose compliance, and treatment intensity for each treatment group in the Safety Population.

## Data Source
- **ADSL** (Subject-Level Analysis Dataset) with exposure variables derived from SDTM EX (Exposure) domain
- Key ADSL variables used:
  - TRTSDT (Date of First Exposure to Study Treatment)
  - TRTEDT (Date of Last Exposure to Study Treatment)
  - TRTDUR (Duration of Treatment in Days)
  - EXDOSE (Dose per Administration)
  - EXSTDTC (Start Date/Time of Exposure)
  - EXENDTC (End Date/Time of Exposure)
  - EXADJ (Reason for Dose Adjustment)
  - EXDOSEU (Dose Units)
  - EXROUTE (Route of Administration)
  - EXDOSFRQ (Dosing Frequency per Interval)

## Display Conventions
- **Treatment Duration**: Calculated as (TRTEDT - TRTSDT + 1) days. Presented as n, mean, SD, median, Q1, Q3, min, max.
- **Dose Compliance**: Calculated as (Actual Total Dose Received / Planned Total Dose) × 100%. Presented as n, mean, SD, median, Q1, Q3, min, max. Compliance categories: <80%, 80-100%, >100%.
- **Treatment Intensity**: Calculated as (Actual Dose Intensity / Planned Dose Intensity) × 100%, where Dose Intensity = Total Dose / Treatment Duration. Presented as n, mean, SD, median, Q1, Q3, min, max.
- **By Group**: Results presented by treatment group (e.g., Placebo, Low Dose, High Dose) and overall.
- **Statistics**: Continuous variables summarized using descriptive statistics (n, mean, SD, median, Q1, Q3, min, max). Categorical variables summarized using counts and percentages.
- **Missing Data**: Subjects with missing exposure data are excluded from relevant summaries and footnoted accordingly.
- **Rounding**: Means and medians rounded to 1 decimal place; SD rounded to 2 decimal places; percentages rounded to 1 decimal place.
- **Footnotes**: Include definition of Safety Population, calculation methods for compliance and intensity, and any protocol deviations affecting exposure.

## Table Shell Layout

| Parameter | Statistic | Placebo (N=xx) | Low Dose (N=xx) | High Dose (N=xx) | Overall (N=xx) |
|-----------|-----------|----------------|-----------------|------------------|----------------|
| **Treatment Duration (Days)** | n | xx | xx | xx | xx |
| | Mean (SD) | xx.x (xx.xx) | xx.x (xx.xx) | xx.x (xx.xx) | xx.x (xx.xx) |
| | Median | xx.x | xx.x | xx.x | xx.x |
| | Q1, Q3 | xx.x, xx.x | xx.x, xx.x | xx.x, xx.x | xx.x, xx.x |
| | Min, Max | xx, xx | xx, xx | xx, xx | xx, xx |
| **Dose Compliance (%)** | n | xx | xx | xx | xx |
| | Mean (SD) | xx.x (xx.xx) | xx.x (xx.xx) | xx.x (xx.xx) | xx.x (xx.xx) |
| | Median | xx.x | xx.x | xx.x | xx.x |
| | Q1, Q3 | xx.x, xx.x | xx.x, xx.x | xx.x, xx.x | xx.x, xx.x |
| | Min, Max | xx.x, xx.x | xx.x, xx.x | xx.x, xx.x | xx.x, xx.x |
| **Compliance Category** | <80% | n (%) | n (%) | n (%) | n (%) |
| | 80-100% | n (%) | n (%) | n (%) | n (%) |
| | >100% | n (%) | n (%) | n (%) | n (%) |
| **Treatment Intensity (%)** | n | xx | xx | xx | xx |
| | Mean (SD) | xx.x (xx.xx) | xx.x (xx.xx) | xx.x (xx.xx) | xx.x (xx.xx) |
| | Median | xx.x | xx.x | xx.x | xx.x |
| | Q1, Q3 | xx.x, xx.x | xx.x, xx.x | xx.x, xx.x | xx.x, xx.x |
| | Min, Max | xx.x, xx.x | xx.x, xx.x | xx.x, xx.x | xx.x, xx.x |

## Footnotes
1. Safety Population includes all subjects who received at least one dose of study treatment.
2. Treatment Duration = (Date of Last Exposure - Date of First Exposure + 1) days.
3. Dose Compliance = (Actual Total Dose / Planned Total Dose) × 100%.
4. Treatment Intensity = (Actual Dose Intensity / Planned Dose Intensity) × 100%, where Dose Intensity = Total Dose / Treatment Duration.
5. Subjects with missing exposure data are excluded from the respective summaries.
6. Percentages are based on the number of subjects in the Safety Population for each treatment group.
7. SD = Standard Deviation; Q1 = First Quartile (25th percentile); Q3 = Third Quartile (75th percentile); Min = Minimum; Max = Maximum.
